name: SWAP 50K Plan (12 weeks)
author: David Roche & Megan Roche, MD PhD
description: Bundled training plan imported from SWAP_Plans PDF templates.

sessions.pace_description: rest
sessions.notes: rest

sessions.pace_description: 5-10 miles easy with 5 x 20 sec hills fast
sessions.notes: 5-10 miles easy with 5 x 20 sec hills fast. On easy running, think smooth, with quick strides, and no tension in the body. On days you feel great, easy runs can be quicker as long as it feels easy and doesn’t impact recovery. On hill strides, find a moderate grade around 6-8% and run up as fast as you can go without sprinting, jogging down and around for 1-2 min before the next one

sessions.pace_description: 2-3 miles easy warm-up (can always add a couple light
sessions.notes: 2-3 miles easy warm-up (can always add a couple light strides to the warm-up on workout days), 6 x 2 min hills around 5k effort, 5 x 45 sec hills around 5k effort, 2-3 miles easy cool- down. These hills are a major power and VO2 max stimulus with limited impact Optional easy double. All doubles are 20-40 min easy unless otherwise noted. May substitute a treadmill uphill, 20-30 min at 15% grade hiking and/or running, or x-training

sessions.pace_description: 5-10 miles easy
sessions.notes: 5-10 miles easy. Post-workout days are very relaxed, nothing is too slow for aerobic development! You can sub in x- training on any Thursday if you feel excessively sore or tired.

sessions.pace_description: 3-6 miles very easy or rest/x- train
sessions.notes: 3-6 miles very easy or rest/x- train. The day before the weekly long run, take it extra chill! You can even shuffle at your slowest paces if tired

sessions.pace_description: 8-14 miles easy/mod
sessions.notes: 8-14 miles easy/mod. On easy/mod long runs, you can go on feel, letting your body dictate effort, with the option to push a bit more on uphills. Fuel all long runs well! Long runs ideally on trails like the race when possible, but roads or other terrain work until specific efforts later

sessions.pace_description: 6-10 miles easy with 4 x 30 second hills
sessions.notes: 6-10 miles easy with 4 x 30 second hills. Sundays ideally on trails as well, but any fun terrain works! Sundays are adventure flex days when you can add or substitute x- training/hiking

sessions.pace_description: rest
sessions.notes: rest

sessions.pace_description: 6-10 miles easy with 5 x 20 sec hills fast
sessions.notes: 6-10 miles easy with 5 x 20 sec hills fast. Think smooth power on the hill strides!

sessions.pace_description: 2-3 miles easy warm-up, 15 x 1 min fast/1 min easy (think
sessions.notes: 2-3 miles easy warm-up, 15 x 1 min fast/1 min easy (think 5k effort), 3 min easy, 5 x 30 sec hills fast, 2-3 miles easy cool-down. On these flat/rolling intervals, maintain high cadence as you fatigue, trying to remove tension from your body as you run fast. On all workouts, err on the side of relaxed and smooth! Optional easy double/treadhill

sessions.pace_description: 6-10 miles easy
sessions.notes: 6-10 miles easy

sessions.pace_description: 3-6 miles very easy or rest/xtrain
sessions.notes: 3-6 miles very easy or rest/xtrain

sessions.pace_description: 10-16 miles easy/mod (20 min mod/hard around threshold
sessions.notes: 10-16 miles easy/mod (20 min mod/hard around threshold effort). After the warm-up, do the tempo around an effort you could sustain for 1 hour, a sweet spot for aerobic and power development.

sessions.pace_description: 8-14 miles easy, aiming to finish feeling fresh, going as
sessions.notes: 8-14 miles easy, aiming to finish feeling fresh, going as slow as needed. First back-to-back, great for resilience!

sessions.pace_description: rest
sessions.notes: rest

sessions.pace_description: 6-10 miles easy with 5 x 20 seconds fast/2 min easy
sessions.notes: 6-10 miles easy with 5 x 20 seconds fast/2 min easy. On these first flat strides, ease into them and work up to the fastest pace you can go without straining, still using long- distance form

sessions.pace_description: 2-3 miles easy warm-up, 6 x 2 min fast/1 min easy (think
sessions.notes: 2-3 miles easy warm-up, 6 x 2 min fast/1 min easy (think 10k effort), 3 min easy, 8 x 1 min fast/1 min easy (think 5k effort), 2-3 miles easy cool- down. These first workouts build speed and power, which will make tempo and long runs more efficient later Optional easy double/treadhill

sessions.pace_description: 6-12 miles easy Optional easy double/treadhill
sessions.notes: 6-12 miles easy Optional easy double/treadhill

sessions.pace_description: 3-6 miles very easy or rest/xtrain
sessions.notes: 3-6 miles very easy or rest/xtrain

sessions.pace_description: 10-16 miles easy/mod (at 20 min and every 5 min after, do 1
sessions.notes: 10-16 miles easy/mod (at 20 min and every 5 min after, do 1 min moderately hard around threshold effort). The run will build up fatigue, but in a fun way for early in a training cycle!

sessions.pace_description: 6-12 miles easy with strong downhills around 50k effort or
sessions.notes: 6-12 miles easy with strong downhills around 50k effort or a bit easier. Downhills are a bit tougher on the body and require adaptation

sessions.pace_description: rest
sessions.notes: rest

sessions.pace_description: 5-8 miles easy with 4 x 20 second hills
sessions.notes: 5-8 miles easy with 4 x 20 second hills. Slight down weekdays to let the body adapt!

sessions.pace_description: 8-12 miles easy/mod, starting easy and settling in at a
sessions.notes: 8-12 miles easy/mod, starting easy and settling in at a steady effort progressing to marathon/50k. This steady running may feel a bit unnatural at first as the body adjust to the new strain Optional easy double/treadhill

sessions.pace_description: 5-8 miles easy
sessions.notes: 5-8 miles easy

sessions.pace_description: 3-6 miles very easy or rest/xtrain
sessions.notes: 3-6 miles very easy or rest/xtrain

sessions.pace_description: 12-18 miles easy/mod (30 min mod/hard around threshold
sessions.notes: 12-18 miles easy/mod (30 min mod/hard around threshold effort). The tempo should feel challenging but manageable!

sessions.pace_description: 8-14 miles easy on trails with 6 x 20 second steep hills
sessions.notes: 8-14 miles easy on trails with 6 x 20 second steep hills. Focus on power on the hills!

sessions.pace_description: rest
sessions.notes: rest

sessions.pace_description: 2-3 miles easy warm-up, 1/2/3/4/3/2/1 min fast with 1 min
sessions.notes: 2-3 miles easy warm-up, 1/2/3/4/3/2/1 min fast with 1 min easy recovery (think 10k effort), 3 min easy, 5 x 1 min fast/1 min easy (think 5k effort), 2-3 miles easy cool-down. This combo workout builds the aerobic system and speed! Optional easy double/treadhill

sessions.pace_description: 5-10 miles easy
sessions.notes: 5-10 miles easy

sessions.pace_description: 6-12 miles easy/mod, starting easy and settling in at a
sessions.notes: 6-12 miles easy/mod, starting easy and settling in at a steady effort. This should start to feel a bit more natural now. Optional easy double/treadhill

sessions.pace_description: 3-6 miles very easy or rest/xtrain
sessions.notes: 3-6 miles very easy or rest/xtrain

sessions.pace_description: 10-16 miles easy/mod (start easy, settle in at steady
sessions.notes: 10-16 miles easy/mod (start easy, settle in at steady effort like 50k or a bit easier). Steady running builds endurance and improves fat oxidation.

sessions.pace_description: 6-12 miles easy on trails with 5 x 30 second hills
sessions.notes: 6-12 miles easy on trails with 5 x 30 second hills

sessions.pace_description: rest
sessions.notes: rest

sessions.pace_description: 6-10 miles easy with 5 x 20 seconds fast/2 min easy
sessions.notes: 6-10 miles easy with 5 x 20 seconds fast/2 min easy. Take this one extra chill before big day on Wed!

sessions.pace_description: 2-3 miles easy warm-up, 5 x 5 min fast (think 10k effort
sessions.notes: 2-3 miles easy warm-up, 5 x 5 min fast (think 10k effort with permission to progress later in workout) with 2 min easy recovery, 4 min easy, 5 x 30 sec hills fast, 2-3 miles easy cool-down. A big one that will bridge to a higher volume of intensity! Optional easy double/treadhill with 2nd half at marathon effort. A double workout, only for very advanced athletes looking for peak performance!

sessions.pace_description: 6-12 miles easy Optional easy double/treadhill
sessions.notes: 6-12 miles easy Optional easy double/treadhill

sessions.pace_description: 3-6 miles very easy or rest/xtrain
sessions.notes: 3-6 miles very easy or rest/xtrain

sessions.pace_description: 14-20 miles easy/mod on trails (12 miles alternating 1 mile
sessions.notes: 14-20 miles easy/mod on trails (12 miles alternating 1 mile at 1-hour effort and 1 mile float recovery). These alternators will be extend the threshold stress over a longer portion of the run, improving aerobic power

sessions.pace_description: 10-14 miles easy on trails with strong downhills
sessions.notes: 10-14 miles easy on trails with strong downhills. Running well on downhills after a tough long run is especially important for races with more climbing

sessions.pace_description: rest
sessions.notes: rest

sessions.pace_description: 2-3 miles easy warm-up, 3 x 8 min fast (think 10k effort)
sessions.notes: 2-3 miles easy warm-up, 3 x 8 min fast (think 10k effort) with 3 min easy recovery, 6 x 1 min slight hills fast with run back recovery (think 5k), 2-3 miles easy. Longer intervals will be tough, and the hills at the end will be a full power stimulus. Optional easy double/treadhill

sessions.pace_description: 6-10 miles easy
sessions.notes: 6-10 miles easy

sessions.pace_description: 6-12 miles easy/mod (at 15 min and every 5 min after, do 1
sessions.notes: 6-12 miles easy/mod (at 15 min and every 5 min after, do 1 min mod/hard around threshold effort) Optional easy double/treadhill

sessions.pace_description: 3-6 miles very easy or rest/xtrain
sessions.notes: 3-6 miles very easy or rest/xtrain

sessions.pace_description: 16-24 miles easy on trails, aiming to finish feeling good
sessions.notes: 16-24 miles easy on trails, aiming to finish feeling good. Fuel well, relax, and gain some aerobic endurance

sessions.pace_description: 12-16 miles easy
sessions.notes: 12-16 miles easy. The aerobic weekend will supercharge endurance heading into final build

sessions.pace_description: rest
sessions.notes: rest

sessions.pace_description: 2-3 miles easy warm-up, 20 min mod/hard around 1-hour
sessions.notes: 2-3 miles easy warm-up, 20 min mod/hard around 1-hour effort, 5 min easy, 6 x 1 min fast/30 seconds easy (think 5k or a bit easier effort), 2-3 miles easy cool- down. Threshold/speed combo before race sim! This serves as a reset week, like the one at week 4 as well Optional easy double/treadhill

sessions.pace_description: 5-10 miles easy
sessions.notes: 5-10 miles easy. Nice little reset before weekend!

sessions.pace_description: 4-8 miles easy or rest/xtrain
sessions.notes: 4-8 miles easy or rest/xtrain

sessions.pace_description: 3-5 miles easy with 4 x 20 second hills
sessions.notes: 3-5 miles easy with 4 x 20 second hills

sessions.pace_description: 1-2 mile easy warm-up, 13
sessions.notes: 1-2 mile easy warm-up, 13.1 miles hard on trails like race, 1-2 mile easy cool-down. This race or race simulation will be a tough stress on every system. You can move this effort to any week between weeks 5 and 10!

sessions.pace_description: Rest or 10 miles easy with strong downhills
sessions.notes: Rest or 10 miles easy with strong downhills. Rest if any fatigue or niggles. The strong downs improve MSK resilience

sessions.pace_description: rest
sessions.notes: rest

sessions.pace_description: 6-12 miles easy with 4 x 20 seconds fast/2 min easy
sessions.notes: 6-12 miles easy with 4 x 20 seconds fast/2 min easy

sessions.pace_description: 2-3 miles easy warm-up, 15 min mod/hard around 1-hour
sessions.notes: 2-3 miles easy warm-up, 15 min mod/hard around 1-hour effort, 5 min easy, 4 x 3 min around 1- hour effort with 1 min easy recovery, 2-3 miles easy cool- down. Major threshold focus! Optional double or treadhill with 20 min around 1-hour effort. Double workout option for supercompensation!

sessions.pace_description: 6-12 miles easy
sessions.notes: 6-12 miles easy

sessions.pace_description: 3-6 miles very easy or rest/xtrain
sessions.notes: 3-6 miles very easy or rest/xtrain
20-26.2 miles easy/mod on trails with strong uphills around 50k effort or a bit easier. This will be the largest aerobic strain of the training block, a time to practice fueling like race day.

sessions.pace_description: 14-20 miles easy on trails with 6 x 20 second hills
sessions.notes: 14-20 miles easy on trails with 6 x 20 second hills. The big back-to-back will make the 50k feel short, and help you truly race it to potential

sessions.pace_description: rest
sessions.notes: rest

sessions.pace_description: 6-12 miles easy plus 4 x 20 second hills
sessions.notes: 6-12 miles easy plus 4 x 20 second hills

sessions.pace_description: 2-3 miles easy warm-up, 2 x (10 x 1 min fast/1 min easy)
sessions.notes: 2-3 miles easy warm-up, 2 x (10 x 1 min fast/1 min easy) with 3 min easy between sets, 2-3 miles easy cool- down. Think 10k effort or so, nice and relaxed as a final speed stimulus! Optional easy double or treadhill

sessions.pace_description: 6-12 miles easy Optional easy double or treadhill
sessions.notes: 6-12 miles easy Optional easy double or treadhill

sessions.pace_description: 3-6 miles very easy or rest/xtrain
sessions.notes: 3-6 miles very easy or rest/xtrain

sessions.pace_description: 16-22 miles easy/mod on trails (1 hour moderate around
sessions.notes: 16-22 miles easy/mod on trails (1 hour moderate around marathon effort to start, faster to finish). The extended tempo will prepare the muscular system for the demands of race day

sessions.pace_description: 10-16 miles easy on trails with strong, purposeful downhills
sessions.notes: 10-16 miles easy on trails with strong, purposeful downhills. Practicing owning the downhills will be free speed on race day.

sessions.pace_description: rest
sessions.notes: rest

sessions.pace_description: 5-10 miles easy with 4 x 20 second hills
sessions.notes: 5-10 miles easy with 4 x 20 second hills

sessions.pace_description: 2-3 miles easy warm-up, 30 min moderate around half
sessions.notes: 2-3 miles easy warm-up, 30 min moderate around half marathon effort, 5 min easy, 4 x 30 second hills fast, 2-3 miles easy cooldown Optional easy double or treadhill

sessions.pace_description: 5-10 miles easy
sessions.notes: 5-10 miles easy

sessions.pace_description: 3-6 miles very easy or rest/xtrain
sessions.notes: 3-6 miles very easy or rest/xtrain

sessions.pace_description: 12-16 miles easy to easy/mod on trails
sessions.notes: 12-16 miles easy to easy/mod on trails. A nice aerobic long run a week before the race activates the nervous and aerobic systems for peak performance.

sessions.pace_description: 6-10 miles easy on trails with 4 x 20 second hills
sessions.notes: 6-10 miles easy on trails with 4 x 20 second hills

sessions.pace_description: Rest
sessions.notes: Rest

sessions.pace_description: 2 miles easy, 20 min at marathon effort, 2 miles easy
sessions.notes: 2 miles easy, 20 min at marathon effort, 2 miles easy

sessions.pace_description: 4-6 miles easy
sessions.notes: 4-6 miles easy

sessions.pace_description: Rest and recovery
sessions.notes: Rest and recovery

sessions.pace_description: 2-4 miles easy plus 4 x 20 second hills mod (not hard) in AM
sessions.notes: 2-4 miles easy plus 4 x 20 second hills mod (not hard) in AM

sessions.pace_description: 50k race! Celebration time! Start relaxed, focus on
sessions.notes: 50k race! Celebration time! Start relaxed, focus on fueling, own the downhills, and make sure to smile a ton. You are READY!

sessions.pace_description: PIZZA! That’s also implied in the whole training plan
sessions.notes: PIZZA! That’s also implied in the whole training plan
